About

Storyteller.
Strategist.
Analyst.

I'm a North Adelaide–based marketing and content professional currently completing a Masters of Data Science at Adelaide University. I work across film production, digital content, brand strategy, and data analytics.

I believe great content starts with understanding the numbers behind it — and that data only becomes useful when someone can translate it into a clear story.

Currently open to part-time, contract, and freelance roles in marketing, content, or data.

I
Film & Content
I

Film & Industry Content

Angaston Tourism Film · SA Hydrogen Conference

ACE Productions · Angaston, Barossa Valley · 2024

A Town Film. $1,000 Budget. 26,000 Views.

Commissioned and paid $1,000 to direct, film, and edit a promotional reel for the town of Angaston. Shot on location with no additional production spend. The film reached an audience more than eight times the size of the town's social following, driven entirely by organic shares.

The film captured Angaston's landscape, vineyards, and community character. It became the anchor piece of the town's digital presence, posted on the Angaston Facebook page.

434 shares meant the video was actively passed between people — not just seen. That's the difference between reach and resonance.

Watch on Facebook →

SA-H2H Hydrogen Technology Cluster · Adelaide · 2025

South Australian
Hydrogen Conference

Provided on-the-ground photography and content capture for SAHY 2025 at the Hilton Adelaide — South Australia's flagship hydrogen industry conference.

Content was used across SA-H2H's digital channels and conference communications, covering ministerial addresses, networking sessions, and the delegate experience.

Featured speaker: The Hon. Tom Koutsantonis MP, Minister for Energy and Mining.

Content & Design

Content & Design

BIMcontent.com · IGS Group · Jun–Dec 2023

BIMcontent.com / IGS Group · 6 months · 2023

40+ Brands. One Content Engine.

As Marketing Content Specialist at IGS Group, I managed social media content and graphic design for BIMcontent.com — a platform connecting building product manufacturers with architects and designers through BIM assets.

Over six months I created content for 40+ brands including Seima, Lysaght, Porter's Paints, Taubmans, Wattyl, Danpal, Electrolux, and Plungie. Output: 40+ Canva designs per month across social scheduling and analytics reporting.

III
Data Science
III

Data Science

Masters Programme · University of Adelaide · 2024–2026

Masters of Data Science · Adelaide University

Where Marketing Meets the Model

Alongside my industry work, I'm completing a Masters of Data Science at Adelaide University — building a rigorous foundation in statistical modelling, data wrangling, and predictive analytics applied to real datasets.

My coursework covers the full pipeline: cleaning messy raw data in the tidyverse through to fitting logistic regression models that predict outcomes with measurable accuracy. The skill is the same as marketing analytics — ask the right question, find the signal, tell someone what it means.

Assignment 1 — Data TamingCleaned a GAA football dataset: renamed columns, removed duplicates, corrected typos, pivoted wide to long, extracted variables via regex.
Assignment 2 — Linear RegressionModelled player medical costs against on-field hours. Box-Cox transformations. Adjusted R² = 0.997.
Assignment 3 — Logistic RegressionBinary injury prediction model. Backwards stepwise selection. AUC = 0.706.
# Logistic regression — injury prediction model question10_glm <- logistic_reg() %>% set_engine("glm") %>% fit(inj ~ age + height + weight + hours + age:weight + age:hours, data = question6_train) question16_preds %>% roc_auc(.pred_ta, truth = inj, event_level = "second") ## AUC: 0.706

Reducing a 37-year-old player's field time from 7 to 3.5 hours per season dropped predicted injury probability from 58.6% to 45.6% — a concrete, actionable output from a statistical model.
